findings.summary: The CONVERSIONS dispatch table wires the converters backwards: ('C','F') maps to f_to_c and ('F','C') to c_to_f, so every cross-unit conversion runs in the wrong direction even though both functions are correct
The new tests assert the inverted behavior (convert(100,'C','F') == 37.78 and convert(100,'F','C') == 212) — they lock in the dispatch bug instead of catching it
